task: grasp the drawer handle and close it
raw:
**SCENE ANALYSIS -** A white drawer is open, revealing a brown interior. A black handle is visible on the right side of the drawer. The drawer is attached to a white cabinet. The drawer is made of wood and has a metallic handle. STRATEGY - First, reach for the drawer handle to grasp it. Then, pull the drawer towards the cabinet to close it. Failure modes include not grasping the handle properly or pulling the drawer in the wrong direction. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1 reach the drawer handle for a secure grip; Step 2 grasp the handle to initiate the pull; Step 3 pull the drawer towards the cabinet to close it.

Goal: closed(drawer)
Scene: drawer, cabinet, handle
Step 1: reach(drawer)
  affordance_region: the black handle of the drawer
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, drawer, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, drawer) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(drawer)
  affordance_region: the black handle of the drawer
  contact: holding(drawer) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(drawer)
Step 3: pull(drawer -> cabinet)
  affordance_region: the area on the cabinet where the drawer should be closed
  contact: surface_contact(drawer, cabinet) [completion]; released(drawer) [completion]
  pose: closed(drawer) [completion]
  done_when: surface_contact(drawer, cabinet) AND released(drawer)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the black handle of the drawer
the black handle of the drawer
the area on the cabinet where the drawer should be closed
